title: pisum sativum subsp. macrocarpum
intro: Pisum sativum subsp. macrocarpum, also known as garden pea or field pea, is a subspecies of the pea plant that produces large, edible seeds. The medicinal properties of Pisum sativum subsp. macrocarpum include its high levels of fiber, protein, and various vitamins and minerals, which have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues and support overall health. The health benefits of consuming Pisum sativum subsp. macrocarpum include its ability to lower cholesterol levels, regulate blood sugar, and provide antioxidant properties that can help protect against cell damage. Pisum sativum subsp. macrocarpum can be prepared as a herbal supplement in the form of capsules, tea, or powder, and is also commonly consumed as a fresh vegetable or dried legume.
